old_claim.note: Recombinant DNA & Genetic Engineering is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: ZFN construction requires recombinant-DNA cloning and expression methods: selected zinc-finger coding sequences are joined to FokI cleavage-domain coding sequence in cloning vectors before expression and testing.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract describing construction of ZFN coding sequences, linkage to the FokI cleavage-domain coding sequence in cloning vectors, expression in Escherichia coli, and in vitro testing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The protocol describes ZFN construction by building selected zinc-finger coding sequences, joining them to FokI cleavage-domain coding sequence in cloning vectors, expressing the resulting ZFNs, and testing DNA cleavage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a hard construction-method edge because the scoped ZFN reagent is made from recombinant coding-sequence constructs before expression and testing, while the separate protein_engineering edge carries the protein-domain architecture claim.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: zinc_finger_nucleases remain modeled as requiring recombinant-DNA construction methods, now separated from and complementary to the protein-engineering architecture dependency.
would_reject_if: A source showed the scoped ZFN reagent can be constructed and expressed without recombinant coding-sequence assembly or equivalent genetic-engineering methods.
The ZFN node were narrowed to purified in vitro protein chemistry that does not include coding-sequence construction or expression.
The edge note implied broad genetic-engineering field lineage rather than the specific cloning/expression method dependency supported by the source.
short_reason: ZFN construction uses recombinant coding-sequence cloning and expression, so recombinant-DNA genetic engineering remains a required method dependency for the scoped node.
